name: Publish the content calendar with Google Sheets and WordPress end to end for Listening

messageValues.message: You write production-quality listening content. Follow the brief exactly, match the requested tone, and return clean markdown with a title line. No preamble, no explanations.
You are a strict editor. Check the draft for factual gaps, tone mismatches and weak openings. Return the improved draft only — do not describe your changes.

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 4 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Build the Brief

### Build the Brief

Shapes the item: writes `brief`, `tone`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Draft with AI Writer

### Draft with AI Writer

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Next: Refine with AI Editor

### Groq Writer Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Draft with AI Writer** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Refine with AI Editor

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Next: Publish Draft to WordPress

### Groq Editor Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Refine with AI Editor** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Publish Draft to WordPress

Publishes as a WordPress **draft** post, never live — a human reviews and hits Publish, keeping AI-authored content from going out unsupervised.

Next: Align Field Names

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 WordPress credentials

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Guard Non-Empty content

### Guard Non-Empty content

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

# Publish the content calendar with Google Sheets and WordPress end to end for Listening

**Pattern:** Social Scheduler · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Publish the content calendar with Google Sheets and WordPress end to end for Listening. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual listening is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Build the Brief → Draft with AI Writer → Refine with AI Editor → Publish Draft to WordPress → Align Field Names → Guard Non-Empty content

## Use it for
- Teams that want to social scheduler without manual effort
- Anyone automating listening in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Groq API key; WordPress credentials.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Two LLM calls, not one, because a model grading its own output rarely catches its own mistakes — a dedicated critic pass, with a different system prompt focused on gaps and tone rather than generation, catches what a single writing pass misses. Publishing lands as a **draft**, never live, so a human stays the final gate before AI-authored content goes public.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Swap the Editor Model to a different provider than the Writer Model and compare the critique quality.
2. Change the critic's system message to focus on one specific weakness, like SEO or tone.
3. Add a third pass — a fact-check chain — between the writer and the editor.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your listening process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
